title: Superseded asymmetric reference parameter spelling
summary: Before DEC-003, concrete references retained written const while deduced auto references used a compact const-default binding spelling.
details: const Foo& ro previously became ro: const Foo&.
Foo& rw became rw: mut Foo&.
Pointer parameter constness has additional independent axes: pointee constness and pointer-object constness cannot be collapsed without a complete rule.

provenance.note: Current concrete and auto parameter branches implement different const-default spellings.
History identifies the separate introductions, but legacy motivations are not treated as current authority.

evidence.reference: tests/param_flip_spec.lua and tests/frontend_spec.lua lambda parameter cases
evidence.claim: Current spellings are pinned for concrete and deduced references.

aliases: const concrete ref versus auto ref
questions: Why does const Foo& retain const but const auto& does not?
How are mutable reference parameters emphasized?
Is current parameter const behavior intentional or only observed?

last_verified.method: Direct code reading and focused test execution; no legacy documentation assumed authoritative.
